edges.description: Mosaic GNAQ/GNA11 signaling increases melanocyte burden in affected pigmentary tissues.

nodes.description: Altered migration or persistence of neural crest-derived melanoblasts during embryogenesis is modeled as an upstream developmental event that places melanocyte precursors in facial dermis and ocular tissues along trigeminal distributions.

evidence.reference_title: An update on ophthalmological perspectives in oculodermal melanocytosis (Nevus of Ota).
evidence.snippet: ODM is a rare, prevalently unilateral, congenital condition that presents with brown or blue/gray flat asymptomatic lesions of the skin, mucosae, episclera/sclera, and uvea localized within the territory of distribution of the ophthalmic and mandibular branches of the trigeminal nerve.

nodes.description: Melanocytes accumulate in facial skin and ocular tissues, producing asymptomatic brown or blue-gray lesions of skin, mucosae, episclera or sclera, and uvea.

evidence.reference_title: An update on ophthalmological perspectives in oculodermal melanocytosis (Nevus of Ota).
evidence.snippet: ODM is a rare, prevalently unilateral, congenital condition that presents with brown or blue/gray flat asymptomatic lesions of the skin, mucosae, episclera/sclera, and uvea localized within the territory of distribution of the ophthalmic and mandibular branches of the trigeminal nerve.

nodes.description: Usually unilateral brown or blue-gray flat facial pigmentation affects skin in trigeminal nerve distributions.

evidence.reference_title: An update on ophthalmological perspectives in oculodermal melanocytosis (Nevus of Ota).
evidence.snippet: ODM is a rare, prevalently unilateral, congenital condition that presents with brown or blue/gray flat asymptomatic lesions of the skin, mucosae, episclera/sclera, and uvea localized within the territory of distribution of the ophthalmic and mandibular branches of the trigeminal nerve.

nodes.description: Glaucoma is a major ophthalmic complication of nevus of Ota.

evidence.reference_title: An update on ophthalmological perspectives in oculodermal melanocytosis (Nevus of Ota).
evidence.snippet: Glaucoma and predisposition to uveal melanoma are the main ophthalmic complications.

evidence.reference_title: Naevus of Ota: clinical characteristics and proposal for a new ocular classification and grading system
evidence.snippet: Three patients developed malignant transformation to choroidal melanoma and four patients developed glaucoma.

nodes.description: Ocular melanocytosis can be complicated by glaucoma, so patients require ophthalmic assessment and ongoing surveillance.

evidence.reference_title: An update on ophthalmological perspectives in oculodermal melanocytosis (Nevus of Ota).
evidence.snippet: Glaucoma and predisposition to uveal melanoma are the main ophthalmic complications.

evidence.reference_title: Naevus of Ota: clinical characteristics and proposal for a new ocular classification and grading system
evidence.snippet: Three patients developed malignant transformation to choroidal melanoma and four patients developed glaucoma.

nodes.description: Postzygotic activating mutations in GNAQ or GNA11 can drive extensive dermal melanocytosis through mosaic activation of heterotrimeric G-protein signaling and MAPK-family pathways in melanocyte lineages.

evidence.reference_title: Mosaic Activating Mutations in GNA11 and GNAQ Are Associated with Phakomatosis Pigmentovascularis and Extensive Dermal Melanocytosis.
evidence.snippet: Here, we discover that extensive dermal melanocytosis and phakomatosis pigmentovascularis are associated with activating mutations in GNA11 and GNAQ, genes that encode Gα subunits of heterotrimeric G proteins.

evidence.reference_title: Mosaic Activating Mutations in GNA11 and GNAQ Are Associated with Phakomatosis Pigmentovascularis and Extensive Dermal Melanocytosis.
evidence.snippet: In vitro expression of mutant GNA11(R183C) and GNA11(Q209L) in human cell lines demonstrated activation of the downstream p38 MAPK signaling pathway and the p38, JNK, and ERK pathways, respectively.

evidence.reference_title: Mosaic Activating Mutations in GNA11 and GNAQ Are Associated with Phakomatosis Pigmentovascularis and Extensive Dermal Melanocytosis.
evidence.snippet: Transgenic mosaic zebrafish models expressing mutant GNA11(R183C) under promoter mitfa developed extensive dermal melanocytosis recapitulating the human phenotype.

nodes.description: Pigmentation can involve episclera or sclera and uveal structures of the affected eye.

evidence.reference_title: An update on ophthalmological perspectives in oculodermal melanocytosis (Nevus of Ota).
evidence.snippet: ODM is a rare, prevalently unilateral, congenital condition that presents with brown or blue/gray flat asymptomatic lesions of the skin, mucosae, episclera/sclera, and uvea localized within the territory of distribution of the ophthalmic and mandibular branches of the trigeminal nerve.

evidence.reference_title: Mosaic Activating Mutations in GNA11 and GNAQ Are Associated with Phakomatosis Pigmentovascularis and Extensive Dermal Melanocytosis.
evidence.snippet: Here, we discover that extensive dermal melanocytosis and phakomatosis pigmentovascularis are associated with activating mutations in GNA11 and GNAQ, genes that encode Gα subunits of heterotrimeric G proteins.

evidence.reference_title: Mosaic Activating Mutations in GNA11 and GNAQ Are Associated with Phakomatosis Pigmentovascularis and Extensive Dermal Melanocytosis.
evidence.snippet: The mutations were detected at very low levels in affected tissues but were undetectable in the blood, indicating that these conditions are postzygotic mosaic disorders.

nodes.description: Uveal melanoma is a serious malignancy risk in ocular or oculodermal melanocytosis.

evidence.reference_title: Ocular and oculodermal melanocytosis associated with uveal melanoma.
evidence.snippet: Fifteen patients with ocular or oculodermal melanocytosis were found after reviewing 1210 cases of histologically proven uveal melanomas.

nodes.description: Ocular or oculodermal melanocytosis increases the risk of uveal melanoma in the affected eye, with tumor development localizing to melanocytotic tissue in reported series.

evidence.reference_title: Ocular and oculodermal melanocytosis associated with uveal melanoma.
evidence.snippet: The melanoma in each of these patients developed in the eye affected with ocular or oculodermal melanocytosis and not in the unaffected eye.

evidence.reference_title: Ocular and oculodermal melanocytosis associated with uveal melanoma.
evidence.snippet: A comparison of the prevalence of ocular or oculodermal melanocytosis in patients with uveal melanoma with the prevalence of ocular or oculodermal melanocytosis in the general population, implies that there is an increased incidence of uveal melanomas in patients with ocular or oculodermal melanocytosis.
